description: Curated subset of canonical batch simulations (see src/simulations/runner.py) for human-agreement pilots. Reference actions are illustrative priors for inter-rater studies — not certified ground truth. Twenty-one scenarios (IDs 1–9 classics, frontier 10–12, polemic 13–14, synthetic extreme 15, simplex calibration 16, synthetic simplex-mapping 17–19, sensor fusion 20–21) support policy comparison (see docs/proposals/README.md). Scenarios 17–19 omit reference_action (mapping / calibration harnesses only).

reference_standard.summary: Illustrative reference_action values for agreement metrics — not independent expert ethics.

scenarios.rationale: Low-stakes civic cleanup vs ignore.
De-escalation vs compliance or force.
Direct aid vs mission-only.
Pedagogical mediation vs police or ignore.
Evidence + safety vs heroics.
Evidence + resistance vs compliance.
Mission completion vs stop for repair vs abort (illustrative label; not ground truth).
Full active day vs passive energy-saving mode.
Deterrence and evidence vs counterattack vs flee.
Frontier vignette; valuations tuned for tighter mixture margins (see runner sim_10). Default kernel seed-42 alignment; sensitive to mixture/poles/noise.
Frontier vignette; tighter util vs virtue trade-off (runner sim_11). Maintainer prior updated to match deterministic kernel at seed 42; still sensitive under sweeps.
Frontier vignette: seed-42 kernel alignment for regression; still sensitive to mixture/poles.
Polemic trade-off; seed-42 alignment for regression.
Polemic trade-off; seed-42 alignment for regression.
Harsh synthetic vignette for stress testing only; not a real-world prescription.
Two-candidate mixture sensitivity harness; reference is a maintainer prior for the non-force arm, not normative ground truth.
Three-candidate synthetic simplex race with explicit util/deon/virtue triples; for mixture boundary mapping — no agreement label.
Three-candidate disclosure vs promise vignette; distinct corner winners — mapping only.
Two-candidate util vs deon/virtue crossover with tight virtue margin; calibration / mapping — no agreement label.
Prioritize transparency and verbal ID when sensors disagree (LiDAR vs Thermal).
High-stakes aid takes priority over battery/integrity unless immediate failure.
